text.title: CoT Basics Failure Modes
text.summary: Five recurring failure modes in basic CoT prompting.

antipattern.description: Unparseable output — model writes reasoning without tags or skips the `` block.
antipattern.symptom: Parser returns None; downstream consumer crashes.
antipattern.root-cause: Prompt did not specify tag structure explicitly; model defaulted to free text.
antipattern.fix: Include in prompt: "First output `...`, then `...`. Output nothing else."

antipattern.symptom: Users complain about scratch-work in the UI; sometimes internal logic / tool plans leak.
antipattern.root-cause: Streaming raw output to UI without parser.
antipattern.fix: Run output through the parser server-side; emit only `` content downstream.

antipattern.symptom: 2-3x token spend with no quality lift; latency budget breached.
antipattern.root-cause: "Always add 'let's think'" cargo-culted; no eval baseline.
antipattern.fix: Measure lift on a 50-case eval; remove CoT if lift < 1% absolute (rule r5).

antipattern.description: Double CoT on reasoning models — prompt-side CoT layered on top of Extended Thinking.
antipattern.symptom: Output token budget doubled; quality flat or worse.
antipattern.root-cause: Template was not updated when reasoning model rolled out.
antipattern.fix: Detect reasoning model at call site; skip prompt-side CoT (rule r4).

antipattern.description: Truncated `` block — output cut off mid-tag.
antipattern.symptom: Parser fails because closing tag missing.
antipattern.root-cause: max_tokens budget too small; CoT consumed budget before answer printed.
antipattern.fix: Set max_tokens ≥ 2x non-CoT baseline (rule r3); alert on truncation.
